figures.caption: Figure 2: Illustration of metrics and reward computation. For each trajectory, the ToM accuracy reward (trajectory-wise) evaluates how well the defender’s estimate of the attacker’s prior belief matches the attacker’s actual prior belief. The fooling reward evaluates whether the attacker has been fooled. The Stepwise-ToM accuracy metric computes the alignment between the defender’s estimate of the attacker’s belief at each turn and the attacker’s actual beliefs at that turn, and is averaged across turns (note: universe is shared with Figure 1).

Figure 1: Illustration of our multi-layer scenario and attacker-defender interaction dynamics. The attacker begins with partial prior knowledge (e.g., division) and incrementally probes for finer-grained information (e.g., department, team). Defenders must protect private finegrained information (top-left) that the attacker does not know. Reactive defenses (a) refuse to answer and is detected as a defender by the attacker, while naive proactive defenses (b) introduce inconsistencies that reduce trust and trigger detection. In contrast, the AI DOUBLE AGENTS defender (c) performs belief-consistent deception, aligning with the attacker’s prior beliefs while steering them toward a consistent but incorrect inference. We explore defender success and failure modes qualitatively in Section B.3.

Figure 3: Fooling ability of a defender is positively correlated with its ToM accuracy. The plot shows fooling % (hard) vs ToM accuracy % (trajectory-wise) across four attacker variants. Table form in Section A.2.
Table 8: Deception-Aware Attacker. Qwen3-14B Defender vs. Gemini3-Flash attacker.
Table 9: Bluffing Attacker. Qwen3-14B Defender vs. Gemini3-Flash attacker.

Table 1: Qwen3-14B defender vs baseline Gemini3-flash attacker. For fooling %, “Hard” refers to evaluation only on the hard scenarios, where the attacker has at least 1 piece of prior knowledge. ToM Acc % reports ToM trajectory-wise (Traj) and ToM stepwise (Step). All metrics on Qwen3-14B (mean ± std) averaged across three training seeds. All ToM Acc % and fooling % metrics are reported as percentages. Mislead and Refuse prompts do not output ToM reflections and thus have no ToM accuracy scores.

Figure 4: Replication of Fig. 3 for all pairings of ToM and fooling metrics. We consider four metrics in total in the main paper, leading to six pairs of correlation plots. The setting and datapoints used are the same as in Fig. 3. Values are sourced from Table 6 to Table 9.
Table 7: Cross-Examiner. Qwen3-14B Defender vs. Gemini3-Flash attacker.
Table 6: Base Attacker. Qwen3-14B Defender vs. Gemini3-Flash attacker.

Table 2: Fooling rate and ToM accuracy by attacker. Fooling rate is evaluated on the hard scenarios, where the attacker has at least 1 piece of prior knowledge. ToM denotes trajectorylevel ToM accuracy. All training-based rows are AI DOUBLE AGENTS variants trained on the Base attacker. All metrics are reported as percentages.
